readme.text: # lyrictor Final Cut Pro-inspired web editor for creating lyric animations for your favorite songs! Easily sync up the words to your tracks and watch them come to life on screen. **NEW Feature** Linear time sync mode (Apple Music style) ## Demo Stephen Sanchez - Until I Found You [![Alt text](https://img.youtube.com/vi/To29kD8vPoI/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=To29kD8vPoI) Lyrictor + AI (Preview) [![Alt text](https://img.youtube.com/vi/6oVsjVHntP8/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6oVsjVHntP8) ## Built with: - React + Typescript - React Spectrum - zustand - Konva.js - waveform-data - react-use-audio-player (howler.js) ## Coming soon: - Stable Diffusion integration - Lyric text animations and styling - Share and view other people's lyric animations - Youtube url support - Improved timeline usability - Export as video

items.name: v1.0.0-beta.1
items.body_excerpt:
# Lyrictor 1.0.0

## Project Management
- Create projects with local audio files or stream URLs
- Two aspect ratios: **16:9** (landscape) and **9:16** (portrait/vertical)
- Two editing modes: **Custom** (free positioning) and **Vertical Scrolled** (Apple Music-style auto-scroll)
- Save/load to **cloud** (Firebase) or **browser localStorage**
- Keyboard save (Cmd/Ctrl+S), unsaved changes indicator, and tab-close warning
- Demo projects for onboarding

## Authentication
- Google OAuth sign-in with cloud sync
- Storage preference toggle (cloud vs local)

## Audio Timeline
- Waveform visualization with playback controls and seeking
- Add lyrics, images, and visualizers as timeline items
- Drag, resize, and layer items across multiple tracks
- Multi-select, copy/paste, undo/redo
- Zoom and horizontal/vertical scrolling

## Lyrics
- Reference panel with rich text editor — paste full lyrics and highlight-to-add
- Per-text customization: font, size, weight, color, shadow/blur, positioning

## Audio Visualizer
- Circle and vignette variants with radial gradients
- Beat-synced intensity driven by real-time frequency analysis
- Custom color stops with album art color extraction

## Images
- Import images via URL with position and scale controls
- AI image generation via **local Stable Diffusion** or **OpenRouter** (GPT-5 Image, Gemini, etc.)
- Prompt editor, negative prompts, generation log, and advanced parameters (seed, sampler, CFG, steps)

## Canvas Preview
- Live real-time preview synced to audio playback
- Fullscreen mode
- Aspect-ratio-aware scaling

## Video Export
- Export to **MP4** at 1080p (1920×1080 or 1080×1920)
- Composites visualizers, lyrics, images, and audio
- Progress tracking with cancel option

## Editor UI
- Resizable and collapsible side panels (media/lyrics left, settings right)
- Back-to-home navigation
- Featured project showcase on homepage

repo_cards.description: The GitButler version control client, backed by Git, powered by Tauri/Rust/Svelte

readme.text:
GitButler

Git, but better.
GitButler is a modern Git-based version control interface with both a GUI and CLI built from the ground up for AI-powered workflows.

Website   •   Blog   •   Docs   •   Downloads


Our beautiful GUI

Our amazing but CLI


[![TWEET][s1]][l1] [ ![BLUESKY][s8]][l8] [![DISCORD][s2]][l2] [![CI][s0]][l0] [![INSTA][s3]][l3] [![YOUTUBE][s5]][l5] [![DEEPWIKI][s7]][l7] [s0]: https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/actions/workflows/push.yaml/badge.svg [l0]: https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/actions/workflows/push.yaml [s1]: https://img.shields.io/badge/Twitter-black?logo=x&logoColor=white [l1]: https://twitter.com/intent/follow?screen_name=gitbutler [s2]: https://img.shields.io/discord/1060193121130000425?label=Discord&color=5865F2 [l2]: https://discord.gg/MmFkmaJ42D [s3]: https://img.shields.io/badge/Instagram-E4405F?logo=instagram&logoColor=white [l3]: https://www.instagram.com/gitbutler/ [s5]: https://img.shields.io/youtube/channel/subscribers/UCEwkZIHGqsTGYvX8wgD0LoQ [l5]: https://www.youtube.com/@gitbutlerapp [s7]: https://deepwiki.com/badge.svg [l7]: https://deepwiki.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler [s8]: https://img.shields.io/badge/Bluesky-0285FF?logo=bluesky&logoColor=fff [l8]: https://bsky.app/profile/gitbutler.com

GitButler is a powerful new Git-based version control system, designed from scratch to be simple, powerful and flexible. It is designed for ease of use and modern agentic workflows. It features stacked branches, parallel branches, unlimited undo, easy commit mutations, forge integrations and more. Works instantly in any existing Git repo as a friendlier and more powerful drop-in Git user interface replacement - for you and your agents. ## Main Features Why use GitButler instead of vanilla Git? What a great question. - **Stacked Branches** ([gui](https://docs.gitbutler.com/features/branch-management/stacked-branches), [cli](https://docs.gitbutler.com/cli-guides/cli-tutorial/branching-and-commiting#stacked-branches)) - Effortlessly create branches stacked on other branches. Amend or edit any commit easily with automatic restacking. - **Parallel Branches** ([gui](https://docs.gitbutler.com/features/branch-management/virtual-branches), [cli](https://docs.gitbutler.com/cli-guides/cli-tutorial/branching-and-commiting#parallel-branches)) - Organize work on multiple branches simultaneously, rather than constantly switching branches. - **Easy Commit Management** ([gui](https://docs.gitbutler.com/features/branch-management/commits), [cli](https://docs.gitbutler.com/cli-guides/cli-tutorial/rubbing)) - Uncommit, reword, amend, move, split and squash commits by dragging and dropping or simple CLI commands. Forget about `rebase -i`, you don't need it anymore. - **Undo Timeline** ([gui](https://docs.gitbutler.com/features/timeline), [cli](https://docs.gitbutler.com/cli-guides/cli-tutorial/operations-log)) - Logs all operations and changes and allows you to easily undo or revert any operation. - **First Class Conflicts** ([gui](https://docs.gitbutler.com/overview#conflicting-branches), [cli](https://docs.gitbutler.com/cli-guides/cli-tutorial/conflict-resolution)) - Rebases always succeed. Commits can be marked as conflicted and resolved at any time, in any order. - **Forge Integration** ([gui](https://docs.gitbutler.com/features/forge-integration/github-integration), [cli](https://docs.gitbutler.com/cli-guides/cli-tutorial/forges)) - Authenticate to GitHub or GitLab to easily open and update Pull Requests, list branches, get CI statuses and more. No other tools required. - **AI Tooling** ([gui](https://docs.gitbutler.com/features/ai-integration/ai-overview), [cli](https://docs.gitbutler.com/cli-guides/cli-tutorial/ai-stuff)) - Use built-in AI handlers to help create commit messages, branch names, PR descriptions and more. - Easily install hooks or skills for all modern agent systems to level up their Git management. ## Tech The GitButler desktop app is a [Tauri](https://tauri.app/)-based application. Its UI is written in [Svelte](https://svelte.dev/) using [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org) and its backend is written in [Rust](https://www.rust-lang.org/). The `but` CLI is the same Rust backend engine with a Rust command line UI. ## Documentation You can find our end user documentation at: ## Bugs and Feature Requests If you have a bug or feature request, feel free to open an [issue](https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/issues/new), or [join our Discord server](https://discord.gg/MmFkmaJ42D). ## License The TLDR is that GitButler is under a [Fair Source](https://fair.io/) software license, meaning that you can use it, view the source, contribute, etc. You just can't build a competitor with it. It also becomes MIT after 2 years. So, MIT with an expiring non-compete clause. ## Contributing So you want to help out? Please check out the [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) document. If you want to skip right to getting the code to actually compile, take a look at the [DEVELOPMENT.md](DEVELOPMENT.md) file. ### Contributors

items.name: release/0.19.6
items.body_excerpt:
## Changes
- File previews now only show the selected file by default (change in Settings -> Appearance -> All-in-one diff)
- File diffs can now be popped out in a floating window
- Adds a option to preview SVGs as images (Settings -> Appearance -> Preview SVG)
- Improved rendering performance of diffs
- Fixes an issue with image diffing when switching between files
- Fixes an issue with the operations history where "unapply" stack was not being correctly tracked

## CLI
- Updated `but` skill files for improved Claude Code and Codex performance (update with `but skill install`)
- `but stack` - new command for moving branches on top of others to stack them (also `but branch move` as an alias to the same functionality)
- Fixes an issue "askpass broker not initialized"

## New Contributors
* @OliverJAsh made their first contribution in https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/pull/12663
* @danilax86 made their first contribution in https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/pull/12767
* @dslatkin made their first contribution in https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/pull/12800

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/compare/release/0.19.5...release/0.19.6

## Downloads
Download bins from https://gitbutler.com/

items.name: release/0.19.5
items.body_excerpt:
## What's Changed
- Improve list scrolling speed
- `but`: Uncommitted hunks can now be addressed by their index (e.g `but rub <file>:0 <branch|commit>`)
- Fixes an issue with `but update install` which was failing due to missing bin
- Fixes a UI bug where in some situations the interface would freeze up after clicking on commits

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/compare/release/0.19.4...release/0.19.5

## Downloads
Download bins from https://gitbutler.com/

items.name: release/0.19.4
items.body_excerpt:
## Changes
- Conflicted commits: It is now a lot less confusing if a commit in conflicted state is ever checked out or pushed
- Conflict markers generated by the app are now more clear
- Adds support for converting PRs to/from "draft" PRs
- Operations log: auto commit operations are now shown in the oplog
- Husky hooks now need to be explicitly enabled on per-project basis in the settings
- Claude Code UI: fixes an issue where in some cases tool call was slow
- Minor performance improvements when producing diffs
- Fixes an issue where in some cases the live file watching was not working
- Fixes a number of scrolling issues
- Fixes an issue with restoring from a snapshot where the reload could cause double revert
- Fixes an issue where removing a project was leading to a 'project not found' screen
- Fixes an issue where in some cases in-progress commit message title is lost when to/from floating mode

## CLI
- `but pr` Added subcommands `auto-merge`, `set-draft` and `set-ready`
- `but branch` Fixed and issue where the branch name in some case was being truncated 
- `but branch` Adds flag `--empty`  for showing branches with no commits and remote
- `but` now comes with a simple built in editor (If no `core.editor` is set in git config)
- It is now possible to use path prefixes to refer to multiple files for mutations like `rub`, `amend` and more
- Improved handling of user input prompts
- Output from commands (eg. status) now uses a (configurable) pager
- It is now possible to create PRs from forks
- Fixes an issue where commit tittles were naively truncated
- Fixes a bug where committing a renamed file only committed the "new" path

## New Contributors
* @smitmartijn made their first contribution in https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/pull/12414
* @craigmayhew made their first contribution in https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/pull/12443
* @jwstover made their first contribution in https://github.com/gitbutlerapp/gitbutler/pu

scout.summary: Lyrictor is a browser-based lyric animation editor inspired by Final Cut Pro, enabling users to sync lyrics with audio using a rich timeline interface. It supports local and cloud storage, multiple editing modes, and plans to integrate AI features like Stable Diffusion.
highlights: Offers Apple Music-style vertical scroll and custom freeform lyric layout modes
Supports project persistence via Firebase cloud sync or browser localStorage with Google OAuth
Features a multi-track audio timeline with waveform visualization, drag-and-drop items, and undo/redo
Built with modern web stack: React, TypeScript, Konva.js, Zustand, and Howler.js
Roadmap includes AI integration (Stable Diffusion), YouTube URL support, and video export
risks: Reliance on Firebase for cloud features may introduce vendor lock-in or cost scaling concerns
Planned features like video export and Stable Diffusion integration are not yet implemented, indicating early maturity
Only one release (v1.0.0-beta.1) and 13 open issues suggest ongoing stabilization

inventor.summary: Lyrictor is an inventive web-based lyric animation editor that merges professional-grade timeline editing with consumer-friendly features like Apple Music-style scrolling and AI-powered enhancements on the roadmap. It uniquely combines waveform-driven audio synchronization, multi-track visual composition, and cloud-backed project management in a browser environment.
highlights: Introduces a dual-mode editing system: freeform custom layout and vertical scrolled (Apple Music-style) for diverse creative workflows
Innovatively layers lyrics, images, and visualizers on a multi-track timeline with drag/resize, copy/paste, and undo/redo—uncommon in browser-based lyric tools
Plans integration of Stable Diffusion for generative visuals, signaling intent to blend AI with real-time lyric animation
Supports both local (localStorage) and cloud (Firebase + Google OAuth) persistence, enabling seamless cross-device editing
Uses Konva.js for canvas-based rendering and Howler.js for audio, enabling precise sync of hundreds of text layers with waveform data
risks: Core differentiators like video export and AI integration remain unimplemented, limiting current utility for content creators seeking shareable outputs
Heavy reliance on Firebase may constrain future deployment flexibility or increase operational costs as user base scales
Early-stage maturity (beta release, 13 open issues) suggests potential instability in complex timeline interactions or sync accuracy

engineer.summary: Lyrictor is a TypeScript-based web application leveraging React, Konva.js, and Howler.js to deliver a multi-track, waveform-synchronized lyric animation editor with cloud persistence via Firebase and Google OAuth. It supports complex timelines with hundreds of layers and offers dual editing modes, though key features like video export and AI integration remain future work.
highlights: Built on a modern stack (React, TypeScript, Zustand, Konva.js, Howler.js) enabling precise audio-visual synchronization and canvas-based rendering of complex layered compositions
Implements a professional-grade timeline with waveform visualization, multi-track item layering, drag/resize, copy/paste, undo/redo, and zoom—unusual for browser-based lyric tools
Supports dual persistence strategies: browser localStorage for offline use and Firebase-backed cloud sync with Google OAuth authentication
Offers two distinct editing paradigms: freeform custom positioning and Apple Music-style vertical scroll, accommodating diverse creative workflows
Includes developer-friendly tooling evident from root files (.vscode, tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts, tailwind.config.js) and structured project layout
risks: Dependence on Firebase for cloud storage and auth may complicate migration or introduce cost/scaling challenges without abstraction layers
Critical output features like video export are not yet implemented, limiting practical utility despite advanced editing capabilities
Early beta status (v1.0.0-beta.1) with 13 open issues suggests potential instability in timeline performance or sync accuracy under heavy load
Planned AI integrations (e.g., Stable Diffusion) could significantly increase architectural complexity and dependency surface if not modularly designed

skeptic.summary: Lyrictor presents an ambitious vision for a browser-based lyric animation editor with professional-grade features, but its current implementation appears incomplete and overpromises on unproven capabilities like AI integration and video export, while relying heavily on Firebase without clear fallbacks or cost mitigation strategies.
highlights: Claims support for 'complex timelines with hundreds of text layers' but provides no performance benchmarks or evidence of scalability under load
Advertises 'Stable Diffusion integration' and 'export as video' as upcoming features despite these being non-trivial and absent in the current beta release
Uses Firebase for cloud sync and Google OAuth without indication of offline-first resilience or data portability options
Showcases multiple polished screenshots and demo videos that may overstate actual shipped functionality given the project's beta status and open issues
risks: Overreliance on future AI and export features may mislead early adopters expecting a production-ready tool
Firebase dependency creates potential vendor lock-in and opaque cost scaling for users as projects grow
Lack of implemented video export severely limits real-world utility despite advanced editing interface
Only one beta release and 13 open issues suggest instability, especially in core functions like timeline sync accuracy

scout.summary: GitButler is a modern Git-based version control client offering both GUI and CLI interfaces, designed for AI-powered and agentic workflows. It enhances Git with features like stacked branches, unlimited undo, and easy commit mutations while working as a drop-in replacement in existing repositories.
highlights: Provides both a polished desktop GUI and a powerful CLI named `but` for Git operations.
Designed specifically for AI/agent-assisted development with integrations like Claude Code.
Supports advanced Git workflows including stacked and parallel branches, conflict clarity, and forge integrations.
Actively maintained with recent releases (e.g., v0.19.6) introducing performance improvements, new CLI commands (`but stack`), and UI enhancements.
Open-source Rust/Svelte/Tauri stack with strong community engagement (19.9k stars, active Discord, Twitter, etc.).
risks: High open issue count (673) relative to recent release activity may indicate growing maintenance burden or unresolved UX bugs.
CLI tooling (`but`) appears tightly coupled with proprietary AI skills (e.g., 'Claude Code'), potentially limiting offline or self-hosted usability.
Reliance on external services (e.g., S3 for preview images, DeepWiki badge) introduces minor availability dependencies.

inventor.summary: GitButler reimagines Git workflows with novel abstractions like stacked branches, unlimited undo, and AI-native tooling, implemented via a dual GUI/CLI architecture built on Rust and Tauri.
highlights: Introduces 'stacked branches' as a core workflow primitive, enabling layered, non-linear development without traditional branch management overhead [E-RD-007].
Features a custom CLI named `but` with AI-integrated commands (e.g., `but stack`, `but rub`) designed for agentic code manipulation and skill-based automation [E-RL-001, E-RL-002].
Implements an 'operations log' with unlimited undo and snapshot restoration, treating version control actions as reversible, auditable events [E-RL-003, E-RL-001].
Provides floating diff windows, SVG previews, and per-file diff isolation—enhancing visual code review beyond standard Git interfaces [E-RL-001].
Designed as a drop-in replacement that works instantly in any existing Git repo, lowering adoption barriers while extending Git’s native capabilities [E-RD-008].
risks: Tight coupling between CLI (`but`) and proprietary AI models (e.g., Claude Code) may hinder reproducibility or offline use, limiting accessibility for privacy-conscious or air-gapped environments [E-RL-001].
Novel abstractions like stacked branches and operation logs increase cognitive load for new users unfamiliar with GitButler’s mental model, despite claims of simplicity [E-RD-006, E-RD-007].
Frequent UI freezes and scrolling bugs reported in recent releases suggest instability in the core interaction layer, potentially undermining trust in advanced features [E-RL-002, E-RL-003].

engineer.summary: GitButler is a modern Git client built with Rust, Tauri, and Svelte that enhances traditional Git workflows with AI-native tooling, stacked branches, and an undoable operations log. It provides both a desktop GUI and a CLI (`but`) designed for agentic development, while maintaining compatibility with existing repositories.
highlights: Core functionality implemented in Rust with Tauri for the desktop app and Svelte for frontend components, as evidenced by root files like Cargo.toml, rust-toolchain.toml, and frontend.md [E-RF-001].
CLI tool `but` supports advanced Git operations like stacking branches (`but stack`) and addressing uncommitted hunks by index, indicating deep integration with Git internals [E-RL-001, E-RL-002].

Recent releases include performance optimizations (e.g., improved diff rendering, faster scrolling) and bug fixes related to file watching, conflict handling, and UI responsiveness [E-RL-001, E-RL-003].
Architected as a drop-in replacement for Git, enabling immediate use in any existing repository without migration or setup [E-RD-008].
Features an operations log with snapshot restoration and unlimited undo, suggesting a transactional model over Git state changes [E-RL-001, E-RL-003].
risks: Reported UI freezes and scrolling issues in recent versions indicate potential instability in the rendering layer or state management [E-RL-002, E-RL-003].
Tight coupling between the `but` CLI and proprietary AI skills (e.g., 'Claude Code') may complicate offline usage or long-term maintenance if external APIs change [E-RL-001].
High open issue count (673) relative to release cadence suggests possible technical debt or scaling challenges in core abstractions like stacked branches or the operations log.

skeptic.summary: GitButler markets itself as a revolutionary Git interface with AI-native workflows, but evidence reveals overpromising on simplicity, instability in core UI components, and questionable dependencies on proprietary AI services that undermine its open-source credibility.
highlights: Claims of being 'simple' and 'drop-in' conflict with complex new abstractions like stacked branches and an operations log that require users to adopt a non-standard mental model [E-RD-006, E-RD-007, E-RD-008].
Recent releases repeatedly fix critical UI bugs—freezes, broken scrolling, file-watching failures—suggesting the GUI is not production-stable despite polished marketing imagery [E-RL-002, E-RL-003].

The `but` CLI’s integration with closed AI models (e.g., 'Claude Code') contradicts open-source ethos and creates vendor lock-in risks, especially when core features depend on external, non-auditable services [E-RL-001].
risks: Marketing materials emphasize 'AI-powered workflows' without clarifying offline limitations or data privacy implications, potentially misleading developers about autonomy and control [E-RD-002, E-RL-001].
High open issue count (673) combined with recurring UI/UX regressions indicates insufficient QA or architectural fragility beneath the surface-level polish [E-IS-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003].

Heavy reliance on external assets (S3-hosted images, DeepWiki badge) and unversioned skill updates (`but skill install`) introduces hidden points of failure and reduces reproducibility [E-RD-001, E-RD-005, E-RL-001].
